response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with a genuine tooling and developer-experience bent (test runners, assertion libraries, prior C/C++ work on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments), but the role's core requirements are largely absent: there is no Kotlin experience, no visible Spring or enterprise Java framework work, and Java appears only peripherally in his history. His depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM ecosystem, so despite real IDE-adjacent interest, the gap on the required stack is too wide for a senior JVM-tooling position.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling instincts: authored test runners, assertion libraries, and DX-focused open source
Broad, long senior-level track record across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput systems
concerns: No Kotlin experience and no evidence of deep JVM expertise
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java framework background
Primary depth is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the Java ecosystem the role centers on
